name: RABBIT-FEATURE-BACKLOG-6
title: Wave-2 deferred: invariant numbering monotonic-order — Inv 35 → 33 → 34 reorder
description:
Deferred from wave-1 housekeeping (BACKLOG-5 scope was already large).

Spec.md numbering ordering drift: Inv 35 (BUG-6 read-comprehend-write, added in wave-1) appears in document order BEFORE Inv 33 and Inv 34. It is filed under the 'Absorbed from rabbit-spec' subsection (conceptually correct) but numerically jumps over the rabbit-feature-new/audit subsections that hold 33/34. A sequential reader encounters 32 -> 35 -> 33 -> 34.

Resolution: renumber sequentially (preferred — clean monotonic order) OR add an explicit numbering callout/note explaining the gap.

Risk: renumbering changes invariant identity. Tests + cross-feature refs that cite specific invariant numbers must update. Grep across the whole .claude/ tree for 'Inv 33', 'Inv 34', 'Inv 35' before renumbering.

This is companion to RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-29 (same kind of structural debt in rabbit-cage spec). If the new monotonic-order enforcement test from BACKLOG-29 is added at the contract level, this becomes a simple 'apply the fix' cycle rather than designing a new check.

Acceptance: rabbit-feature spec.md invariants in monotonic document order; all cross-references to renumbered invariants updated; new enforcement test (from RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-29) passes for this feature.
